title: Corynandra simplicifolia
intro: Corynandra simplicifolia, commonly known as the False Jasmine Vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Corynandra simplicifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as being used in folk medicine to promote wound healing and reduce fever.
taxonomy: Corynandra simplicifolia is classified in the family Capparaceae, commonly known as the caper family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are native to various parts of the world. The Capparaceae family consists of approximately 940 species, which are often found in tropical and subtropical regions.
